meta.title: ILOILO CITY
meta.subtitle: Iloilo Province, Western Visayas
meta.description: Iloilo City is a vibrant heritage destination known as the "Queen City of the South," featuring centuries-old Spanish colonial architecture, ancestral houses, and a thriving culinary scene. The city seamlessly blends rich cultural traditions with modern development, making it a premier destination for history enthusiasts and food lovers. Home to the legendary Dinagyang Festival and finest seafood, Iloilo City offers authentic Filipino charm and urban sophistication.

attractions.name: Miagao Church
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage baroque church featuring intricate stone carvings and distinctive architectural design.
attractions.things_to_do: Photography, Historical tours, Architecture appreciation, Attend mass, Local crafts shopping

attractions.name: Jaro Cathedral
attractions.description: One of the oldest cathedrals in the Philippines with stunning Gothic-inspired architecture and historical significance.
attractions.things_to_do: Attend services, Photography, Historical exploration, Spiritual visits, Architecture study

attractions.name: Fort San Pedro
attractions.description: Historic Spanish colonial fortress representing Iloilo's maritime heritage and military significance from the 16th century.
attractions.things_to_do: Historical tours, Photography, Educational visits, Sunset viewing, Museum exploration

attractions.name: Nelly Garden Museum
attractions.description: A charming museum showcasing ancestral house interiors, antiques, and artifacts representing Iloilo's colonial past.

attractions.description: Historic public plaza hosting the iconic Dinagyang Festival and serving as the heart of Iloilo's cultural celebrations.
